long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: Example of vinylic halide is:
questions.solution_summary: Vinylic halides have halogen atom attached to sp² hybridised carbon of a C=C double bond. Option 2 represents vinyl chloride (CH₂=CH-Cl) which is a vinylic halide.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of vinylic halide definition and examples.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements. Statement (I): The gas liberated on warming a salt with dil H₂SO₄, turns a piece of paper dipped in lead acetate into black, it is a confirmatory test for sulphide ion. Statement (II): In statement-I the colour of paper turns black because of formation of lead sulphite.
questions.sub_topic: Sulphide ion test - lead acetate paper
questions.solution_summary: H₂S gas (liberated from sulphide salt with dil H₂SO₄) reacts with lead acetate to form black PbS (lead sulphide), not lead sulphite. Statement I is true, Statement II is false.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of confirmatory test for sulphide ion.

questions.question_text: This reduction reaction is known as: C₆H₅COCl → H₂/Pd/BaSO₄ → C₆H₅CHO
questions.solution_summary: Rosenmund reduction converts acid chlorides to aldehydes using H₂/Pd/BaSO₄ (poisoned catalyst).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of Rosenmund reduction.

questions.question_text: The final product A, formed in the following multistep reaction sequence is:
questions.solution_summary: Following the reaction sequence involving bromination, dehydrohalogenation, oxidation and other transformations, product A is identified as option 1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction sequence for product identification.

questions.question_text: In the given reactions, identify the reagent A and reagent B:
questions.solution_summary: The correct reagents for the given transformations are identified based on the reaction products.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of reagents for functional group transformations.

questions.question_text: Match List-I with List-II: List-I (Species): Cr²⁺, Mn⁺, Ni²⁺, V⁺. List-II (Electronic distribution): 3d⁸, 3d⁴s¹, 3d⁴, 3d⁴s¹.
questions.sub_topic: Electronic configurations of transition metal ions
questions.solution_summary: Cr²⁺: [Ar]3d⁴ (A-III); Mn⁺: [Ar]3d⁵4s¹ (B-IV); Ni²⁺: [Ar]3d⁸ (C-I); V⁺: [Ar]3d³4s¹ (D-II).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching of transition metal ions with their electronic configurations.

questions.question_text: Diamagnetic Lanthanoid ions are: (1) Nd³⁺ & Ce⁴⁺ (2) La³⁺ & Ce⁴⁺ (3) Nd³⁺ & Eu³⁺ (4) Lu³⁺ & Eu³⁺
questions.solution_summary: La³⁺ = 4f⁰ (no unpaired electrons, diamagnetic). Ce⁴⁺ = 4f⁰ (diamagnetic). Nd³⁺ = 4f³ (paramagnetic), Eu³⁺ = 4f⁶ (paramagnetic), Lu³⁺ = 4f¹⁴ (diamagnetic but paired with Eu³⁺ makes it wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of f-electron configurations and magnetic properties.

questions.question_text: Sugar which does not give reddish brown precipitate with Fehling's reagent, is: (1) Maltose (2) Glucose (3) Lactose (4) Sucrose
questions.solution_summary: Sucrose is a non-reducing sugar because its aldehyde/ketonic groups are involved in glycosidic linkage. Maltose, glucose and lactose are reducing sugars.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of reducing and non-reducing sugars.

questions.question_text: Match List-I with List-II: List-I (Molecule): BrF₅, H₂O, ClF₃, SF₄. List-II (Shape): T-shape, Bent, See-saw, Square pyramidal.
questions.solution_summary: BrF₅ → Square pyramidal (A-IV); H₂O → Bent (B-III); ClF₃ → T-shape (C-I); SF₄ → See-saw (D-II).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching of molecules with shapes based on VSEPR theory.

questions.question_text: Structure of 4-Methylpent-2-enal is:
questions.solution_summary: 4-Methylpent-2-enal: CH₃-CH(CH₃)-CH=CH-CHO. The aldehyde group is at C-1, double bond at C-2, methyl at C-4.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of IUPAC nomenclature and structure drawing.

questions.question_text: Compound A formed in the following reaction reacts with B gives the product C. Find out A and B: CH₃-C≡CH + Na → A → B → CH₃-C≡C-CH₂-CH₂-CH₃ (C).
questions.sub_topic: Alkyne reactions - formation of higher alkynes
questions.solution_summary: CH₃-C≡CH + Na → CH₃-C≡CNa⁺ (A). Reaction with 1-bromopropane (B) gives CH₃-C≡C-CH₂-CH₂-CH₃ (C).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of alkyne acidity and alkylation.

questions.question_text: Choose the correct statements from the following: (A) Ethane-1,2-diamine is a chelating ligand. (B) Metallic aluminium is produced by electrolysis of aluminium oxide in presence of cryolite. (C) Cyanide ion is used as ligand for leaching of silver. (D) Phosphine act as a ligand in Wilkinson catalyst. (E) The stability constants of Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ are similar with EDTA complexes.
questions.solution_summary: A: True (en is bidentate chelating ligand). B: True (Al extracted by electrolysis of Al₂O₃ with cryolite). C: True (CN⁻ leaches Ag from ores). D: False (Wilkinson catalyst has triphenylphosphine, not phosphine). E: False (Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ have different stability constants with EDTA).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of multiple concepts in coordination chemistry and metallurgy.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: Statement (I): The orbitals having same energy are called as degenerate orbitals. Statement (II): In hydrogen atom, 3p and 3d orbitals are not degenerate orbitals.
questions.solution_summary: Statement I is true (degenerate orbitals have same energy). Statement II is false (in hydrogen atom, 3s, 3p and 3d all have same energy).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of degeneracy in hydrogen atom.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: Assertion (A): CH₂=CH-CH₂-Cl is an example of allyl halide. Reason (R): Allyl halides are the compounds in which the halogen atom is attached to sp² hybridised carbon atom.
questions.solution_summary: Assertion (A) is true (allyl halide). Reason (R) is false (in allyl halides, halogen is attached to sp³ hybridised carbon adjacent to sp² carbon).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of allyl halide definition and hybridisation.

questions.question_text: Following is a confirmatory test for aromatic primary amines. Identify reagent (A) and (B).
questions.solution_summary: Aromatic primary amine + NaNO₂/HCl (0-5°C) → diazonium salt (A). Diazonium salt + β-naphthol in NaOH → scarlet red azo dye (B = β-naphthol, C = NaOH).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of diazotization and azo dye formation.

questions.question_text: The Lassaigne's extract is boiled with dil. HNO₃ before testing for halogens because: (1) Silver halides are soluble in HNO₃ (2) Ag₂S is soluble in HNO₃ (3) Na₂S and NaCN are decomposed by HNO₃ (4) AgCN is soluble in HNO₃
questions.solution_summary: Lassaigne's extract is boiled with dil HNO₃ to decompose Na₂S and NaCN (if present), which would otherwise interfere with the halogen test by forming precipitates with AgNO₃.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of Lassaigne's test procedure for halogen detection.

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): There is a considerable increase in covalent radius from N to P. However from As to Bi only a small increase in covalent radius is observed. Reason (R): Covalent and ionic radii in a particular oxidation state increases down the group.
questions.solution_summary: Assertion (A) is true (large N to P increase, small As to Bi due to d/f orbital screening). Reason (R) is true but is NOT the correct explanation (R is a general statement, doesn't explain the specific anomaly).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of covalent radius trends and anomalies.

questions.question_text: Aluminium chloride in acidified aqueous solution forms an ion having geometry: (1) Octahedral (2) Tetrahedral (3) Square planar (4) Trigonal bipyramidal
questions.sub_topic: Hybridisation and geometry of hydrated Al³⁺
questions.solution_summary: AlCl₃ in acidified aqueous solution forms [Al(H₂O)₆]³⁺. Al³⁺ has sp³d² hybridisation with octahedral geometry.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of hydrated aluminium complex.

questions.question_text: What happens to freezing point of benzene when small quantity of naphthalene is added to benzene? (1) First decreases and then increases (2) Increases (3) Decreases (4) Remains unchanged
questions.solution_summary: Addition of non-volatile solute (naphthalene) to a solvent (benzene) lowers the vapour pressure, causing depression in freezing point.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of freezing point depression by non-volatile solute.

questions.question_text: Which of the following molecule/species is most stable?
questions.solution_summary: Cyclopropenium ion is aromatic (4n+2 π electrons with n=0, all atoms in a ring, planar) and is the most stable among the given species.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of aromaticity and stability comparison.

questions.question_text: On a thin layer chromatographic plate, an organic compound moved by 3.5 cm, while the solvent moved by 5 cm. The retardation factor of the organic compound is ____ × 10⁻¹.
questions.solution_summary: Rf = distance travelled by solute / distance travelled by solvent = 3.5/5 = 0.7 = 7 × 10⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Simple calculation of Rf value from TLC data.

questions.question_text: 2MnO₄⁻ + bI⁻ + cH₂O → xI₂ + yMnO₂ + zOH⁻. If the above equation is balanced with integer coefficients, the value of z is ____.
questions.solution_summary: Balanced equation: 2MnO₄⁻ + 6I⁻ + 4H₂O → 3I₂ + 2MnO₂ + 8OH⁻. So z = 8.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Balancing redox equation in basic medium.

questions.question_text: 0.05 cm thick coating of silver is deposited on a plate of 0.05 m² area. The number of silver atoms deposited on plate are ____ × 10²³. (At mass Ag = 108, d = 7.9 g cm⁻³)
questions.solution_summary: Volume = area × thickness = 0.05 m² × 0.05 cm = 500 cm² × 0.05 cm = 25 cm³. Mass = density × volume = 7.9 × 25 = 197.5 g. Moles = 197.5/108 = 1.83 mol. Atoms = 1.83 × 6.022 × 10²³ = 11.02 × 10²³ ≈ 11 × 10²³.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step calculation involving volume, mass, moles and atoms.

questions.question_text: An ideal gas undergoes a cyclic transformation starting from the point A and coming back to the same point by tracing the path A→B→C→A as shown in the diagram. The total work done in the process is ____ J.
questions.solution_summary: Work done = area under the PV curve (cyclic) = ½ × (volume change) × (pressure change). From the diagram, area = ½ × 0.02 × 20000 = 200 J.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Work done calculation from PV diagram.

questions.question_text: The mass of sodium acetate (CH₃COONa) required to prepare 250 mL of 0.35 M aqueous solution is ____ g. (Molar mass of CH₃COONa is 82.02 g mol⁻¹)
questions.solution_summary: Moles = Molarity × Volume (in L) = 0.35 × 0.250 = 0.0875 mol. Mass = moles × molar mass = 0.0875 × 82.02 = 7.176 g ≈ 7 g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Molarity to mass conversion.

questions.question_text: If IUPAC name of an element is 'Unununium' then the element belongs to nᵗʰ group of Periodic table. The value of n is ____.
questions.solution_summary: Unununium = 111 (un=1, un=1, un=1). Atomic number 111 belongs to group 11 (coinage metals: Cu, Ag, Au, Rg).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Conversion of IUPAC nomenclature to atomic number and group identification.

questions.question_text: The compound formed by the reaction of ethanol with semicarbazide contains ____ number of nitrogen atoms.
questions.solution_summary: Ethanol reacts with semicarbazide (H₂N-NH-C(=O)-NH₂) to form semicarbazone. Semicarbazone contains 3 nitrogen atoms (two from hydrazine part, one from amide part).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of semicarbazone structure and nitrogen count.

questions.question_text: The total number of molecular orbitals from 2s and 2p atomic orbitals of a diatomic molecule is ____.
questions.solution_summary: 2s-2s combination gives 2 MOs (σ₂s and σ*₂s). 2p-2p combination gives 6 MOs (σ₂pz, σ*₂pz, π₂px, π₂py, π*₂px, π*₂py). Total = 2 + 6 = 8 MOs.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of number of MOs formed from 2s and 2p orbitals.

questions.question_text: The rate of First order reaction is 0.04 mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹ at 10 minutes and 0.03 mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹ at 20 minutes after initiation. Half life of the reaction is ____ minutes. (Given: log 2 = 0.3010, log 3 = 0.4771)
questions.sub_topic: First order kinetics - rate and half-life

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of first order kinetics to determine rate constant and half-life from rate data.

questions.question_text: The pH at which Mg(OH)₂ [Ksp = 1×10⁻¹¹] begins to precipitate from a solution containing 0.10 M Mg²⁺ is ____.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Ksp to find pH of precipitation.
